creator: se@FreeBSD.org
text:
Portmaster has code to detect whether a package install is explicitly requested or automatic because of some dependency. In the latter case, -DINSTALL_DEPENDS is passed to "make install" (look for the only occurrence of INSTALL_DEPENDS in portmaster to locate that code).

I do not have time to verify the correct working of that logic, currently. Since I have concentrated on adding FLAVOR support, since taking over maintainership, I'm not sure under which exact conditions INSTALL_DEPENDS is passed. The situation is complicated by the recursive invocation of portmaster with synthesized parameters for actual compiles. Since portmaster does not check the state of the automatic-flag in ports being updated, I do not see how that information can be passed to the install phase, currently. (I.e., the initial installation may have been marked as automatic, but I guess that information is lost when the automatic port is later upgraded.)

I'm working on a re-implementation of the build strategy logic in portmaster, which will get rid of the recursion. I'll implement correct management of the autoinstall flag, in that version. But that version will require a few more months to make it feature-complete.

If you depend on "pkg autoremove" working now, your best approach is probably to use poudriere to build packages, which will then be correctly registered by pkg during installation and upgrades.
